comments.author: @TruePlanetOffical
comments.text: SOUTH DAKOTA: Forgotten Wonderland at the Edge of America | 4K Travel Documentary

comments.author: @garyreeve6793
comments.text: The AI voice is annoying and on top of that it's not an American voice

replies.author: @TruePlanetOffical
replies.text: Thanks for the honest feedback. We know the narration isn't for everyone, and we're always looking at ways to improve the viewing experience. We appreciate you giving the video a try. If you enjoy our travel documentaries, please consider subscribing, liking, and sharing—it really helps us keep making better content.

comments.author: @robertcornelius3514
comments.text: Is he pointing to an empty whiskey bottle?

replies.author: @TruePlanetOffical
replies.text:
No, that’s not an empty whiskey bottle. That’s the Crazy Horse Memorial in South Dakota. He’s pointing toward the horizon, symbolizing his vision for his people and their future.
Have you had a chance to visit the Black Hills yet?

comments.author: @montaratravel
comments.text: Outstanding visuals. I've spent quite a bit of time filming there myself, and every visit reveals something new. It's one of those places you can never fully explore.

replies.author: @TruePlanetOffical
replies.text: Hey, thanks so much! That means a lot coming from someone who's filmed there themselves. It's one of those magical places that always surprises you. What's one of your favorite moments or discoveries you've captured on camera?

comments.author: @BeautifuLakesStreamsBiologists
comments.text: I consulted on land outside Deadwood  SD.  Gorgeous area!   It is a plaese can create such beauty

replies.author: @TruePlanetOffical
replies.text: Thanks for sharing that! Deadwood really is a special place, and I'm glad you got to experience its beauty firsthand. What stood out to you the most while you were there?

comments.author: @DonovanPhillips-h8l
comments.text: Awesome 🎉Thank you

replies.author: @DonovanPhillips-h8l
replies.text: ​@TruePlanetOfficalEverything to be honest. Thanks

replies.author: @TruePlanetOffical
replies.text: Thanks so much! I really appreciate you being here. 😊 What was your favorite part of the video?

replies.author: @TruePlanetOffical
replies.text: I hear you, and I get why you feel strongly about it. It’s a tough balance when people need the land to work, but it also needs to be protected. What do you think is the biggest problem right now—greed from companies, or lack of rules being enforced?

comments.author: @raijatilles1099
comments.text: 😢😢😢history is so sad😢

replies.author: @TruePlanetOffical
replies.text: @raijatilles1099 I understand exactly what you mean. Their story is one of the hardest parts of our history, and it's important that it continues to be told with honesty and respect. Thank you for sharing your thoughts. Have you ever visited any historic sites that left a similar impression on you?

replies.author: @raijatilles1099
replies.text: ​@TruePlanetOfficali feel for the indigenous peoples and how they trust was betrayed 😢

replies.author: @TruePlanetOffical
replies.text: Thank you for watching. ❤️ History can be heartbreaking, but I also think it's important that we remember these stories so they aren't forgotten. Was there a particular part of the video that moved you the most?

comments.author: @Laurie-b5c
comments.text: Yes have visited dozens of times, love it. LOVE RAPID CITY!  Great place we go through on our way to the mnts. I can drive there in my sleep, I have gone through so many times!

replies.author: @montaratravel
replies.text: That's awesome! It sounds like Rapid City never gets old. 😄 But let's keep the "driving in your sleep" part just a figure of speech!

comments.author: @raymora512
comments.text: Evem the UFOs  dom't go there ...ad lads ...

replies.author: @TruePlanetOffical
replies.text: 😂 Haha, that gave me a good laugh! Maybe even the UFOs are keeping this place a secret. 😄 If you had to send them anywhere on Earth, where would it be?

comments.author: @EpicEarthYTB
comments.text: South Dakota’s landscapes are absolutely breathtaking! I loved learning about the geological history of the Badlands and seeing the majestic bison in their natural habitat.

replies.author: @TruePlanetOffical
replies.text: Thank you so much! South Dakota really is full of incredible surprises. The Badlands almost feel like another planet, and seeing bison roaming freely is something you never forget. If you could spend one day there, would you choose to explore the Badlands or drive through Custer State Park?

comments.author: @dantv23
comments.text: The monument mentioned is the geographic center of the North American continent, not of all 50 states as incorrectly stated.

replies.author: @TruePlanetOffical
replies.text: @dantv23 Thank you so much for watching and for taking the time to join the conversation. It really means a lot! If you enjoyed the video and think others might too, I'd truly appreciate it if you shared it with someone who loves exploring America. Thanks again, and safe travels on your next adventure!

replies.author: @dantv23
replies.text: @TruePlanetOfficalNo, but when I get back into nomadic living I would make it a possible visit stop. Thanks for your contact as well.

replies.author: @TruePlanetOffical
replies.text: Thanks for catching that! You're absolutely right—I should have said the geographic center of the North American continent, not the center of all 50 states. I appreciate you pointing it out, and I'll be more careful with details like that in future videos. Out of curiosity, have you ever visited the monument yourself?

comments.author: @StephenScottHughes
comments.text: Grew up in South Dakota so this doc really hits different for me. The scenery looks stunning but that aint millions of years, its from Noahs flood waters. Still loved the footage tho!

replies.author: @TruePlanetOffical
replies.text: Thanks for watching, and I really appreciate hearing from someone who grew up in South Dakota. It's always interesting to see how people interpret the history behind these incredible landscapes. While viewers may have different perspectives on how these formations came to be, I'm glad the scenery and footage resonated with you. Thanks for sharing your thoughts and being part of the conversation!

comments.author: @larrypaSrker
comments.text: The Bible makes it clear God created the world, its not 75 million years old. This old earth talk in the video is just misinformation.

replies.author: @TruePlanetOffical
replies.text: Thank you for sharing your thoughts. People approach this topic from different perspectives, and respectful discussion is always welcome here. I appreciate you watching and taking part in the conversation.

comments.author: @BradleyDavidAlvarez
comments.text: We visited last September 2025 and Custer State Park blew us away. We hit all the main spots and it was incredible. Already planning our November trip back, thanks for the awesome documentary!

replies.author: @TruePlanetOffical
replies.text: Thank you so much for watching! Custer State Park is truly one of those places that exceeds every expectation. I'm glad you were able to experience the main highlights, and it's awesome to hear you're already planning another trip. November should offer a completely different atmosphere and scenery. Wishing you an amazing return visit, and thanks for supporting the channel!

comments.author: @matthewgonzalez1997
comments.text: few lil mistakes but theyre cute lol 😅 still a great watch tho

replies.author: @TruePlanetOffical
replies.text: Thank you! 😄 We definitely had a few small mistakes slip through, and I appreciate you pointing them out with such a good sense of humor. We're always learning and improving with every video. Glad you still enjoyed the watch!

comments.author: @BrandonDonaldWilson
comments.text: South Dakota is so beutiful 😍 Been wanting to go back and just explore more for ages now. This video is fire!

replies.author: @TruePlanetOffical
replies.text: Thank you so much! 😊 South Dakota really is one of those places that surprises people with just how much natural beauty it has. There’s still so much I want to explore there myself. If you could go back tomorrow, what’s the first place you’d visit?

comments.author: @NicolasDudic-ph4kd
comments.text: Amazing vidéo Thanks!

replies.author: @TruePlanetOffical
replies.text: Thank you so much! I'm really glad you enjoyed the video. Your support and kind words mean a lot, and I appreciate you taking the time to watch and comment. 😊

comments.author: @WanderWorld-x6m
comments.text: great video!

replies.author: @TruePlanetOffical
replies.text:
Thank you so much! I'm really glad you enjoyed the video. 😊
I'd love to hear your thoughts as well—was there anything you think I could improve or add to make future videos even better? Your feedback is always appreciated!

comments.author: @phaedrussmith1949
comments.text:
Hesapa kin le Wakhan thanka makha kin lo chokanon ahignaka cha he ikce wichasa le nazinpi kin lena thawapo lo.

The sacred black hills were created by god for the indigenous people who live here.
#stillHere #Landback

replies.author: @TruePlanetOffical
replies.text: @jillwoodward3519 Good question. That shot was meant to help illustrate the broader journey into the American interior. I honestly didn't think anyone would notice it, but you caught it right away. Thanks for pointing it out!

replies.author: @jillwoodward3519
replies.text: Why did you show film of St.Louis and the Arch in a story about South Dakota??  Especially when you didn’t even comment on it? Makes no sense.

replies.author: @TruePlanetOffical
replies.text: @phaedrussmith1949 Thank you, that means a lot coming from someone with such a deep connection to this land. I truly appreciate your kind words. Our hope is to help more people discover and understand places like this. If you feel the video did it justice, I'd be honored if you shared it with others.

replies.author: @phaedrussmith1949
replies.text: ​@TruePlanetOfficalI think you did a good job. Thank you for remembering us and having the courage to speak the truth of this land.

replies.author: @TruePlanetOffical
replies.text:
@phaedrussmith1949  Thank you for watching and for taking the time to share your thoughts. I really appreciate your perspective.
When you watched my video, is there anything you’d like us to improve or do differently in future documentaries? Your feedback would mean a lot.

replies.author: @phaedrussmith1949
replies.text: ​@TruePlanetOfficalMy people have been here for thousands and thousands of years. We will be here for thousands more.

replies.author: @TruePlanetOffical
replies.text: Thank you for your comment. I’m sorry, but I don’t understand the first part written in your native language. Would you mind explaining it in English so I can better understand your perspective? I’d love to learn more about the connection between the Black Hills and the people who call it home.
